records.ety: [Ir. pillin, pilliun (akin to Gael. pillean, pillin), fr. Ir. & Gael. pill, peall, a skin or hide, prob. fr. L. pellis. See Pell, n., Fell skin.]

defs: A panel or cushion saddle; the under pad or cushion of saddle; esp., a pad or cushion put on behind a man's saddle, on which a woman may ride.
quotes: His [a soldier's] shank pillion without stirrups.

defs: A saddle or seat behind the driver's saddle on a motorcycle, used by passengers.
